questions.question: Which of the following best describes the Amazon SageMaker Canvas ML tool?

choices.text: Provides one-click, end-to-end solutions for many common machine learning use cases
choices.explanation: This describes a different SageMaker capability, not the core purpose of SageMaker Canvas.

choices.text: Gives the ability to use machine learning to generate predictions without the need to write any code
choices.explanation: SageMaker Canvas lets users build machine learning models and generate predictions through a no-code visual interface.

choices.text: Explains how input features contribute to the model predictions during model development and inference
choices.explanation: This describes a different SageMaker capability, not the core purpose of SageMaker Canvas.

choices.text: The fastest and easiest way to prepare tabular and image data for machine learning
choices.explanation: This describes a different SageMaker capability, not the core purpose of SageMaker Canvas.

questions.question: Which security discipline in the Generative AI Security Scoping Matrix focuses on identifying potential threats to generative AI solutions and recommending mitigations?

choices.text: Governance and compliance
choices.explanation: This discipline is important for AI governance, but it is not the one centered on threat identification and mitigation.

choices.text: Legal and privacy
choices.explanation: This discipline is important for AI governance, but it is not the one centered on threat identification and mitigation.

choices.text: Risk management
choices.explanation: Risk management focuses on identifying threats to generative AI solutions and recommending mitigations.

choices.text: Resilience
choices.explanation: This discipline is important for AI governance, but it is not the one centered on threat identification and mitigation.

questions.question: A technology consulting firm is advising a client on how to integrate AI into their customer service and content creation workflows. The client is particularly interested in using Large Language Models (LLMs) for tasks such as automating customer support, generating marketing content, and processing large volumes of text data. To ensure they choose the right applications, the team needs to understand the potential uses of LLMs across different industries and business functions. What do you suggest?

choices.text: LLMs are used for generating human-like text, translating languages, summarizing text, and answering questions based on large datasets
choices.explanation: LLMs are commonly used for text generation, translation, summarization, question answering, and other language tasks.

choices.text: LLMs are used to synthesize realistic human speech from text inputs
choices.explanation: This describes another generative AI modality rather than the primary use cases of large language models.

choices.text: LLMs are used for designing and generating 3D models for use in various applications such as gaming, virtual reality, or industrial design
choices.explanation: This describes another generative AI modality rather than the primary use cases of large language models.

choices.text: LLMs are used for creating videos from textual descriptions
choices.explanation: This describes another generative AI modality rather than the primary use cases of large language models.

questions.question: A financial services company is exploring Amazon Q Business to automate reporting and streamline business insights across departments. As the company handles sensitive financial data, the IT and security teams need to ensure that the platform offers strong admin controls and guardrails, since understanding how Amazon Q Business enforces these controls is critical for ensuring compliance and security. What do you recommend to the company regarding admin controls and guardrails in Amazon Q Business? (Select two)

choices.text: Amazon Q Business guardrails support topic-specific controls to determine the web application environment's behavior when it encounters a mention of a blocked topic by an end-user
choices.explanation: Amazon Q Business guardrails can use topic-specific controls for blocked topics in the web experience.

choices.text: Amazon Q Business guardrails do not support topic-specific controls to determine the web application environment's behavior when it encounters a mention of a blocked topic by an end-user
choices.explanation: This statement contradicts Amazon Q Business guardrail or response-generation controls.

choices.text: Amazon Q Business chat responses can be generated using model knowledge and enterprise data, or enterprise data only
choices.explanation: Amazon Q Business can control whether responses use enterprise data only or both enterprise data and model knowledge.

choices.text: Amazon Q Business chat responses can be generated using only model knowledge
choices.explanation: This statement contradicts Amazon Q Business guardrail or response-generation controls.

choices.text: Amazon Q Business never allows the end users to upload files in chat to generate responses from those uploaded files
choices.explanation: This statement contradicts Amazon Q Business guardrail or response-generation controls.

questions.question: A financial services company relies on several Independent Software Vendors (ISVs) for key operational applications and needs to maintain up-to-date compliance records to meet regulatory requirements. To streamline its compliance management process, the company wants to receive email notifications whenever new ISV compliance reports, such as SOC 2 or ISO certifications, become available, ensuring that its compliance team is promptly informed and can take necessary actions. Which AWS service would be most suitable for automatically providing these notifications?

choices.text: The company should use AWS Config to enable continuous monitoring of AWS resource configurations to ensure compliance with best practices and internal policies. Leverage the ntegration with Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) to receive notifications when the compliance reports are available
choices.explanation: This service is not the best fit for receiving newly available compliance report notifications.

choices.text: The company should use AWS Artifact to facilitate on-demand access to AWS compliance reports and agreements, as well as allow users to receive notifications when new compliance documents or reports, including ISV compliance reports, are available
choices.explanation: AWS Artifact provides access to compliance reports and can notify users when new reports, including ISV reports, are available.

choices.text: The company should use AWS Trusted Advisor to receive notification alerts for best practices and recommendations to optimize AWS resources
choices.explanation: This service is not the best fit for receiving newly available compliance report notifications.

choices.text: The company should use AWS Audit Manager and leverage its integration with Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) to receive notifications when the compliance reports are available
choices.explanation: This service is not the best fit for receiving newly available compliance report notifications.

questions.question: A healthcare company is integrating AWS AI services to build models for patient data analysis and personalized treatment recommendations. The company is exploring the AWS AI service cards and it would like to know more about use cases relevant to AWS AI service cards. Given this context, what would you identify as the primary purpose of AWS AI service cards??

choices.text: To provide detailed technical documentation for setting up AWS AI services
choices.explanation: AI service cards are not setup documentation, a marketplace, or a model-sharing platform.

choices.text: To serve as a marketplace for purchasing third-party AI services using pre-paid cards
choices.explanation: AI service cards are not setup documentation, a marketplace, or a model-sharing platform.

choices.text: To offer transparency and information about the intended use, limitations, and potential impacts of AWS AI services, helping users implement Responsible AI practices
choices.explanation: AWS AI service cards provide transparency about intended use, limitations, responsible design, and potential impacts.

choices.text: To provide a platform for users to share their AI models and datasets with the community
choices.explanation: AI service cards are not setup documentation, a marketplace, or a model-sharing platform.

questions.question: A healthcare company is developing a machine learning model to predict patient outcomes based on medical data. To ensure the model generalizes well, the company needs to understand the balance between underfitting and overfitting and how to address these issues. Which of the following would you identify as correct regarding underfitting and overfitting in machine learning?

choices.text: Underfit models experience high bias, whereas, overfit models experience low variance
choices.explanation: This option reverses or misstates the usual bias and variance behavior of underfitting and overfitting.

choices.text: Underfit models experience low bias, whereas, overfit models experience high variance
choices.explanation: This option reverses or misstates the usual bias and variance behavior of underfitting and overfitting.

choices.text: Underfit models experience high bias, whereas, overfit models experience high variance
choices.explanation: Underfit models typically have high bias, while overfit models typically have high variance.

choices.text: Underfit models experience low bias, whereas, overfit models experience low variance
choices.explanation: This option reverses or misstates the usual bias and variance behavior of underfitting and overfitting.

questions.question: Match the following Amazon SageMaker services to the respective use cases: A) SageMaker Data Wrangler B) SageMaker Canvas C) SageMaker Ground Truth 1) Harnessing human input across the ML lifecycle to improve the accuracy and relevancy of models 2) Offers 300+ pre-configured data transformations to prepare data for ML 3) No-code service with an intuitive, point-and-click interface

choices.explanation: Data Wrangler prepares data, Canvas is a no-code point-and-click ML tool, and Ground Truth uses human input across the ML lifecycle.
This mapping assigns at least one SageMaker service to the wrong use case.
This mapping assigns at least one SageMaker service to the wrong use case.
This mapping assigns at least one SageMaker service to the wrong use case.

questions.question: A retail company is exploring machine learning to improve customer segmentation and discover hidden patterns in sales data. The data science team is particularly interested in using unsupervised learning to analyze large volumes of unlabeled customer and product data to identify trends and groupings without predefined categories. To determine the best approach, they need to understand which methods fall under unsupervised learning. Which of the following would you suggest to the company as examples of unsupervised learning? (Select two)

choices.text: Neural network
choices.explanation: This is not the best example of an unsupervised learning method in this context.

choices.text: Sentiment analysis
choices.explanation: This is not the best example of an unsupervised learning method in this context.

choices.text: Clustering
choices.explanation: Clustering is a common unsupervised learning technique for finding groups in unlabeled data.

choices.text: Dimensionality reduction
choices.explanation: Dimensionality reduction is a common unsupervised learning technique for discovering compact structure in unlabeled data.

choices.text: Decision tree
choices.explanation: This is not the best example of an unsupervised learning method in this context.

questions.question: A healthcare company is implementing a machine learning solution to predict patient outcomes and improve treatment plans. The data science team is working to structure their workflow effectively, ensuring that they follow the correct steps in the machine learning process. Understanding the proper sequence of these steps will help the team streamline their project and ensure a successful implementation. Given this context, which of the following would you recommend as the correct sequence of steps in the machine learning process?

choices.text: Data collection, Data preprocessing, Model training, Model evaluation
choices.explanation: A typical ML workflow starts with data collection, then preprocessing, model training, and model evaluation.

choices.text: Model training, Data collection, Data preprocessing, Model evaluation
choices.explanation: This sequence places core ML workflow steps in the wrong order.

choices.text: Data preprocessing, Model evaluation, Model training, Data collection
choices.explanation: This sequence places core ML workflow steps in the wrong order.

choices.text: Model evaluation, Model training, Data collection, Data preprocessing
choices.explanation: This sequence places core ML workflow steps in the wrong order.

questions.question: A research lab is exploring various generative AI models for its project on creating realistic images and data simulations. The lab is particularly interested in diffusion models but needs a clear understanding of how these models work. Gaining insights into the mechanism behind diffusion models will help the lab decide whether this approach is suitable for their data generation needs. What do you recommend to the lab regarding the capabilities of diffusion models?

choices.text: Diffusion models are a type of transformer-based models that use a self-attention mechanism
choices.explanation: This describes transformers, latent-space models, or GANs rather than diffusion models.

choices.text: Diffusion models create new data by iteratively making controlled random changes to an initial data sample
choices.explanation: Diffusion models generate data through an iterative noise and denoising process that transforms samples step by step.

choices.text: Diffusion models work by learning a compact representation of data called latent space
choices.explanation: This describes transformers, latent-space models, or GANs rather than diffusion models.

choices.text: Diffusion models work by training two neural networks in a competitive manner
choices.explanation: This describes transformers, latent-space models, or GANs rather than diffusion models.

questions.question: A video streaming company is developing machine learning models to recommend content and analyze user interactions. The data science team needs to understand the specific capabilities of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs). Which of the following would you suggest to the team?

choices.text: While RNNs are used for single image analysis, CNNs are used for video analysis
choices.explanation: This option misstates the typical strengths of CNNs and RNNs.

choices.text: Both RNNs and CNNs are used for single image analysis
choices.explanation: This option misstates the typical strengths of CNNs and RNNs.

choices.text: Both RNNs and CNNs are used for video analysis
choices.explanation: This option misstates the typical strengths of CNNs and RNNs.

choices.text: While CNNs are used for single image analysis, RNNs are used for video analysis
choices.explanation: CNNs are commonly used for image analysis, while RNNs are suited to sequential data such as time series or video sequences.

questions.question: A customer service company is exploring ways to improve its AI-powered chatbot, seeking to balance automation with human input to ensure high-quality responses. The company is considering two approaches: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) and Amazon Augmented AI (A2I). However, the company needs to understand the primary differences between these two approaches, as it will help the company choose the right approach to enhance the chatbot's accuracy and reliability. What would you recommend to the company?

choices.text: RLHF is a technique used to train AI models using human feedback to refine their behavior, whereas A2I is an AWS service that provides a human review of machine learning predictions to improve model accuracy and reliability
choices.explanation: RLHF trains model behavior from human feedback, while Amazon A2I adds human review workflows for ML predictions.

choices.text: RLHF is used exclusively for natural language processing tasks, whereas A2I is used for image recognition and analysis tasks
choices.explanation: This option mischaracterizes either RLHF, Amazon A2I, or the role of human review.

choices.text: RLHF requires no human involvement during the training process, while A2I automates the entire machine learning workflow without human review
choices.explanation: This option mischaracterizes either RLHF, Amazon A2I, or the role of human review.

choices.text: RLHF focuses on automatically generating data labels for training datasets, while A2I is used for unsupervised learning tasks
choices.explanation: This option mischaracterizes either RLHF, Amazon A2I, or the role of human review.

questions.question: A retail company is looking to analyze its sales performance over the past 12 months to identify trends, track key performance indicators, and make informed strategic decisions. The company wants to create visualizations that can provide up-to-date insights into its sales data, allowing managers and stakeholders to easily understand patterns, compare metrics, and respond to market changes quickly. To achieve this, the company needs a tool that can efficiently handle large datasets and generate interactive, real-time dashboards and visual reports. Which tool would be most suitable for creating visualizations that meet the company’s objectives?

choices.text: The company should use SageMaker Data Wrangler, as it includes built-in analyses that help you generate visualizations and data analyses in a few clicks
choices.explanation: This tool is not the best fit for interactive BI dashboards over sales data.

choices.text: The company should use Amazon QuickSight, a business intelligence (BI) service that allows users to easily create and share interactive dashboards and visualizations from various data sources, including up-to-date sales data, enabling real-time insights and reporting
choices.explanation: Amazon QuickSight is the BI service for creating and sharing interactive dashboards and visualizations from business data.

choices.text: The company should use SageMaker Canvas, a no-code tool that allows users to build visualizations created by machine learning models
choices.explanation: This tool is not the best fit for interactive BI dashboards over sales data.

choices.text: The company should use CloudWatch Dashboard, which is designed for monitoring and visualizing metrics at scale
choices.explanation: This tool is not the best fit for interactive BI dashboards over sales data.

questions.question: A media analytics company utilizes Amazon Bedrock to run inferences with its generative AI models to analyze large volumes of user-generated content and provide insights to its clients. The company frequently processes numerous inference requests and is looking for a way to minimize the costs associated with running these inferences while still maintaining the required level of service. Given that the company can tolerate some delays in receiving responses, it seeks a cost-effective inference method that optimizes resource usage without sacrificing too much on turnaround time. Which inference approach would be the most suitable for the company to use in order to reduce its overall inference costs?

choices.text: The company should use serverless inference, which automatically scales resources based on traffic
choices.explanation: This inference mode is less suited to reducing cost for delay-tolerant bulk processing.

choices.text: The company should use on-demand inference, which allows the company to pay only for the resources consumed during each inference
choices.explanation: This inference mode is less suited to reducing cost for delay-tolerant bulk processing.

choices.text: The company should use real-time inference, which is designed for low-latency responses and continuous, immediate processing
choices.explanation: This inference mode is less suited to reducing cost for delay-tolerant bulk processing.

choices.text: The company should use batch inference, thereby allowing it to run multiple inference requests in a single batch
choices.explanation: Batch inference is appropriate when many requests can be processed together and the workload can tolerate delayed responses.

questions.question: A consulting firm is considering adopting Amazon Q Business to help its teams automate workflows, generate business insights, and streamline decision-making. To ensure smooth integration with their existing AWS infrastructure, the firm's IT department needs to understand which underlying AWS service powers Amazon Q Business. What do you suggest?

choices.text: Amazon Kendra
choices.explanation: This is not the underlying generative AI service behind Amazon Q Business.

choices.text: Amazon Q Apps
choices.explanation: This is not the underlying generative AI service behind Amazon Q Business.

choices.text: Amazon SageMaker Jumpstart
choices.explanation: This is not the underlying generative AI service behind Amazon Q Business.

choices.text: Amazon Bedrock
choices.explanation: Amazon Q Business uses generative AI capabilities from Amazon Bedrock rather than being powered by the other listed services.

questions.question: A company wants to implement safeguards for its generative AI application using Amazon Bedrock. Specifically, the company wants to filter undesirable and harmful content as well as redact any personally identifiable information (PII). What do you recommend?

choices.text: Watermark detection for Amazon Bedrock
choices.explanation: This Bedrock feature does not provide the requested content filtering and PII redaction safeguards.

choices.text: Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock
choices.explanation: This Bedrock feature does not provide the requested content filtering and PII redaction safeguards.

choices.text: Continued pretraining in Amazon Bedrock
choices.explanation: This Bedrock feature does not provide the requested content filtering and PII redaction safeguards.

choices.text: Guardrails for Amazon Bedrock
choices.explanation: Guardrails for Amazon Bedrock can filter harmful content and help redact sensitive information such as PII.

questions.question: A financial services company is developing a machine learning model to predict credit risk. During the model evaluation, the data science team notices that the model performs exceptionally well on the training data but struggles with new, unseen data, indicating overfitting. To address this issue, the team needs to identify the root cause of overfitting. What would you recommend to the team?

choices.text: Overfitting occurs when the model is overly complex and captures noise or random fluctuations in the training data rather than the underlying patterns
choices.explanation: Overfitting happens when a model learns noise or random fluctuations in the training data instead of general patterns.

choices.text: Overfitting occurs when the model is not updated frequently enough with new data, leading to outdated patterns
choices.explanation: This is not the root cause of a model performing well on training data but poorly on unseen data.

choices.text: Overfitting occurs when the model is using fewer feature combinations
choices.explanation: This is not the root cause of a model performing well on training data but poorly on unseen data.

choices.text: Overfitting occurs when the model ignores the training data and makes predictions based on pre-defined rules
choices.explanation: This is not the root cause of a model performing well on training data but poorly on unseen data.

questions.question: A retail company wants to leverage machine learning to analyze customer behavior and predict future purchasing trends but lacks in-house coding expertise. The company’s goal is to build a model that can identify patterns in customer data and forecast sales, helping to tailor marketing strategies and inventory management. Since the team does not have any programming skills, they are considering different tools or services that would enable them to develop a machine learning model without writing any code. Given this limitation, which of the following tools or services would be most suitable for the company to use?

choices.text: The company should use SageMaker Data Wrangler to simplify data preparation and feature engineering, which are mandatory steps towards building a machine learning model
choices.explanation: This option does not best satisfy the requirement to build models without programming skills.

choices.text: The company should use SageMaker Built-in Algorithms, which provide a collection of pre-built algorithms for building machine learning models
choices.explanation: This option does not best satisfy the requirement to build models without programming skills.

choices.text: The company should use SageMaker Canvas, as it enables users to create machine learning models using a visual interface
choices.explanation: SageMaker Canvas enables users to build ML models with a visual, no-code interface.

choices.text: The company should use SageMaker Clarify, as it enables users to create machine learning models using a visual interface
choices.explanation: This option does not best satisfy the requirement to build models without programming skills.

questions.question: A financial services company is deploying a machine learning model using Amazon Bedrock to predict loan approval risks. The data science team needs to ensure that the model performs effectively before going into production. They are focused on understanding the correct practices and tools for model evaluation on Amazon Bedrock to ensure accuracy, fairness, and reliability in their predictions. Which of the following are correct regarding model evaluation for Amazon Bedrock? (Select two)

choices.text: Human model evaluation is valuable for assessing qualitative aspects of the model, whereas, automatic model valuation is valuable for assessing quantitative aspects of the model
choices.explanation: Human evaluation is useful for qualitative assessment, while automatic evaluation is useful for quantitative metrics.

choices.text: Automatic model evaluation is valuable for assessing qualitative aspects of the model, whereas, human model valuation is valuable for assessing quantitative aspects of the model
choices.explanation: This statement reverses the evaluation types or attributes automatic metrics to the wrong approach.

choices.text: Automatic model evaluation provides model scores that are calculated using various statistical methods such as BERT Score and F1
choices.explanation: Automatic model evaluation can calculate model scores with statistical methods such as BERTScore and F1.

choices.text: For human model evaluation, you can use either built-in prompt datasets or your own prompt datasets
choices.explanation: This statement reverses the evaluation types or attributes automatic metrics to the wrong approach.

choices.text: Human model evaluation provides model scores that are calculated using various statistical methods such as BERT Score and F1
choices.explanation: This statement reverses the evaluation types or attributes automatic metrics to the wrong approach.

questions.question: An organization deploys its IT infrastructure in a combination of its on-premises data center along with AWS Cloud. How would you categorize this deployment model?

choices.text: Cloud deployment
choices.explanation: This term does not describe a combined on-premises and cloud deployment.

choices.text: Hybrid deployment
choices.explanation: Combining on-premises infrastructure with AWS Cloud is a hybrid deployment model.

choices.text: Private deployment
choices.explanation: This term does not describe a combined on-premises and cloud deployment.

choices.text: Mixed deployment
choices.explanation: This term does not describe a combined on-premises and cloud deployment.

questions.question: Is it possible to increase both the bias and variance of a machine learning model simultaneously?

choices.text: Yes, increasing both bias and variance simultaneously will improve the model's accuracy and generalization capabilities
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly treats bias and variance as always improving accuracy or as a strict inverse relationship.

choices.text: No, increasing bias always decreases variance and vice versa, so they cannot be increased at the same time
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly treats bias and variance as always improving accuracy or as a strict inverse relationship.

choices.text: No, it is not possible to increase both bias and variance simultaneously, as they are inversely related
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly treats bias and variance as always improving accuracy or as a strict inverse relationship.

choices.text: Yes, it is possible to increase both bias and variance, but this typically leads to a model that performs poorly due to both underfitting and overfitting
choices.explanation: Bias and variance can both be high in a poorly specified or unstable model, leading to weak generalization.

questions.question: A technology company is developing a machine learning model to automatically categorize images for its e-commerce platform, which includes tasks like identifying products in photos uploaded by users. The data science team is exploring various types of neural networks and needs to choose the most effective one for image classification. Understanding which neural network architecture is best suited for handling the complexities of image data will help the team ensure accurate and efficient classification. What do you recommend for the given use case?

choices.text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
choices.explanation: This architecture or technique is not the best fit for image classification.

choices.text: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
choices.explanation: Convolutional Neural Networks are designed to learn spatial patterns and are commonly used for image classification.

choices.text: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs)
choices.explanation: This architecture or technique is not the best fit for image classification.

choices.text: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs)
choices.explanation: This architecture or technique is not the best fit for image classification.

questions.question: A media company is considering using generative AI to automate content creation for articles, videos, and marketing campaigns. The team wants to understand the underlying mechanics of generative AI, particularly how these models are able to create entirely new content or data, such as text, images, and music, based on patterns learned from existing datasets. This understanding will help the company determine how to best integrate generative AI into its creative workflows. Given this context, how does generative AI create new content or data?

choices.text: By using pre-defined rules and templates without any learning from existing data
choices.explanation: Generative AI does not rely only on fixed rules, manual coding, or random output unrelated to data.

choices.text: Through traditional programming methods where each outcome is manually coded
choices.explanation: Generative AI does not rely only on fixed rules, manual coding, or random output unrelated to data.

choices.text: By randomly generating content without any reference to existing data
choices.explanation: Generative AI does not rely only on fixed rules, manual coding, or random output unrelated to data.

choices.text: By learning patterns from existing data and using algorithms to generate new content that mimics those patterns
choices.explanation: Generative AI learns patterns from existing data and uses those learned patterns to create new content.

questions.question: A customer support company is using Amazon Bedrock to automate responses to frequently asked questions through its AI-driven chatbot. The development team is adjusting various inference parameters to control the responses. They are particularly interested in the Response length parameter, since this parameter is critical for providing clear, customer-friendly interactions. How does the inference parameter Response length influence the model response for Amazon Bedrock?

choices.text: Specifies the minimum or maximum number of tokens to return in the generated response.
choices.explanation: Response length controls the minimum or maximum number of tokens returned in the generated output.

choices.text: Influences the percentage of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token
choices.explanation: This describes another inference parameter such as top-p, stop sequences, or top-k.

choices.text: Specifies the sequences of characters that stop the model from generating further tokens
choices.explanation: This describes another inference parameter such as top-p, stop sequences, or top-k.

choices.text: Influences the number of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token
choices.explanation: This describes another inference parameter such as top-p, stop sequences, or top-k.

questions.question: A retail company is building multiple machine learning models using Amazon SageMaker to optimize inventory management and customer recommendations. The data science teams want to collaborate more effectively by sharing and reusing features without duplicating data across different models. They are looking for a service within Amazon SageMaker that allows them to maintain a centralized catalog of features, ensuring consistency and efficiency in their machine learning workflows. What do you suggest?

choices.text: Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler
choices.explanation: This SageMaker capability does not provide the centralized feature catalog requested.

choices.text: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store
choices.explanation: SageMaker Feature Store provides a centralized repository to store, share, and reuse ML features.

choices.text: Amazon SageMaker Clarify
choices.explanation: This SageMaker capability does not provide the centralized feature catalog requested.

choices.text: Amazon SageMaker Model Dashboard
choices.explanation: This SageMaker capability does not provide the centralized feature catalog requested.

questions.question: A healthcare company is developing a machine learning model to classify medical conditions based on patient data. The data science team needs to evaluate the model’s performance to ensure that it makes correct predictions, particularly for critical diagnoses. To do so, the team is considering various performance metrics commonly used for classification systems. Which of the following performance metrics would you recommend to the team for evaluating the effectiveness of its classification system?

choices.text: Precision, Recall and F1-Score
choices.explanation: Precision, recall, and F1-score are standard metrics for evaluating classification systems.

choices.text: Throughput, Latency and Uptime
choices.explanation: These metrics are better suited to operations, model behavior, or regression rather than classification effectiveness.

choices.text: Bias and Variance
choices.explanation: These metrics are better suited to operations, model behavior, or regression rather than classification effectiveness.

choices.text: Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) and R-squared
choices.explanation: These metrics are better suited to operations, model behavior, or regression rather than classification effectiveness.

questions.question: A software company is looking for tools to help its IT professionals streamline the process of coding, testing, and upgrading applications. The team is evaluating different solutions that can improve efficiency, automate routine tasks, and enhance productivity for its workflow. Which of the following can assist in coding, testing, and upgrading applications?

choices.text: Amazon Q in QuickSight
choices.explanation: This Amazon Q offering targets a different business workflow rather than developer productivity.

choices.text: Amazon Q in Connect
choices.explanation: This Amazon Q offering targets a different business workflow rather than developer productivity.

choices.text: Amazon Q Business
choices.explanation: This Amazon Q offering targets a different business workflow rather than developer productivity.

choices.text: Amazon Q Developer
choices.explanation: Amazon Q Developer assists IT professionals and developers with coding, testing, troubleshooting, and application upgrades.

questions.question: A healthcare company is building a machine learning model to predict patient outcomes based on various health indicators. The data science team is exploring different techniques to improve the model’s accuracy by refining the input data, specifically using feature extraction and feature selection. Understanding the key differences between these two approaches will help the team optimize the model’s performance. What do you suggest to the company?

choices.text: Feature extraction and feature selection are both used to remove irrelevant features but do not reduce the dimensionality of the dataset
choices.explanation: This option reverses, narrows, or misstates feature extraction and feature selection.

choices.text: Feature extraction involves selecting the most relevant features from the dataset, while feature selection involves creating new features from existing data
choices.explanation: This option reverses, narrows, or misstates feature extraction and feature selection.

choices.text: Feature extraction is only applicable to supervised learning, while feature selection is only applicable to unsupervised learning
choices.explanation: This option reverses, narrows, or misstates feature extraction and feature selection.

choices.text: Feature extraction reduces the number of features by transforming data into a new space, while feature selection reduces the number of features by selecting the most relevant ones from the existing features
choices.explanation: Feature extraction creates transformed features, while feature selection keeps the most relevant existing features.

questions.question: A company is considering using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to optimize decision-making in dynamic environments. Which of the following would you recommend as the best-fit use case for Reinforcement Learning?

choices.text: Reinforcement learning is used for making predictions based on historical data trends
choices.explanation: This describes supervised, unsupervised, or regression-style learning rather than reinforcement learning.

choices.text: Reinforcement learning is primarily used for clustering large datasets without any predefined labels
choices.explanation: This describes supervised, unsupervised, or regression-style learning rather than reinforcement learning.

choices.text: Reinforcement learning is used for optimizing complex systems such as robotics, game playing, and industrial automation by learning optimal actions through trial and error
choices.explanation: Reinforcement learning learns actions through trial and error, making it suitable for robotics, games, and control optimization.

choices.text: Reinforcement learning is used for performing regression analysis on large numerical datasets
choices.explanation: This describes supervised, unsupervised, or regression-style learning rather than reinforcement learning.

questions.question: A legal services company is implementing AI solutions using Amazon Bedrock to improve document analysis and automate responses to client queries. The team is exploring two approaches - Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Agents - since understanding the key differences between these approaches will help the company decide which method best suits their AI strategy for delivering precise, context-aware responses. Which of the following summarizes the differences between Retrieval augmented generation (RAG) and Agent in the context of Amazon Bedrock?

choices.text: Both RAG and Agent refer to querying and retrieving information from a data source to augment a generated response to a prompt
choices.explanation: This option confuses retrieval augmentation with agent orchestration.

choices.text: RAG refers to querying and retrieving information from a data source to augment a generated response to a prompt, whereas, Agent refers to an application that carries out orchestrations through cyclically interpreting inputs and producing outputs by using a foundation model
choices.explanation: RAG retrieves external information to augment responses, while an agent orchestrates steps and actions using a foundation model.

choices.text: Both RAG and Agent refer to an application that carries out orchestrations through cyclically interpreting inputs and producing outputs by using a foundation model
choices.explanation: This option confuses retrieval augmentation with agent orchestration.

choices.text: Agent refers to querying and retrieving information from a data source to augment a generated response to a prompt, whereas, RAG refers to an application that carries out orchestrations through cyclically interpreting inputs and producing outputs by using a foundation model
choices.explanation: This option confuses retrieval augmentation with agent orchestration.

questions.question: A company is using Amazon Bedrock based Foundation Model in a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) configuration to provide tailored insights and responses based on client data stored in Amazon S3. Each team within the company is assigned to different clients and uses the foundation model to generate insights specific to their clients' data. To maintain data privacy and security, the company needs to ensure that each team can only access the model responses generated from the data of their respective clients, preventing any unauthorized access to other teams' client data. What is the most effective approach to implement this access control and maintain data security?

choices.text: The company should configure S3 bucket policies to allow access to all teams but monitor usage through AWS CloudTrail logs to detect any unauthorized access
choices.explanation: This approach grants overly broad access or relies on detection instead of enforcing least privilege.

choices.text: The company should create a single IAM policy that grants read-only access to all S3 buckets for all teams
choices.explanation: This approach grants overly broad access or relies on detection instead of enforcing least privilege.

choices.text: The company should create a service role for Amazon Bedrock for each team, granting access only to the specific team's clients data in Amazon S3
choices.explanation: Separate Bedrock service roles scoped to each team’s S3 data enforce least-privilege access for each client dataset.

choices.text: The company should create a single role for Amazon Bedrock with full access to Amazon S3 and then create separate IAM roles for each team that are limited to each team's clients data
choices.explanation: This approach grants overly broad access or relies on detection instead of enforcing least privilege.

questions.question: A social media company is planning to implement a large language model (LLM) for content moderation to automatically flag inappropriate or harmful content. To ensure the model is fair and does not show bias or discrimination against specific groups or individuals, the company needs to evaluate the model's outputs regularly for potential bias. The team is considering different data sources for this evaluation but wants to choose an option that minimizes administrative effort while still providing reliable and comprehensive insights into any biases or discrimination present in the LLM's outputs. Given these requirements, which data source would be most suitable?

choices.text: The company should use benchmark datasets, which are pre-compiled, standardized datasets specifically designed to test for biases and discrimination in model outputs
choices.explanation: Benchmark datasets are standardized and reduce administrative effort while supporting bias and discrimination evaluation.

choices.text: The company should use randomly selected user-generated data, where random samples from actual user interactions are analyzed to identify potential biases
choices.explanation: This option requires more manual effort or is less standardized for bias evaluation.

choices.text: The company should use internally generated synthetic data, which involves creating artificial datasets tailored to specific scenarios
choices.explanation: This option requires more manual effort or is less standardized for bias evaluation.

choices.text: The company should use human-monitored benchmarking, where human reviewers manually assess the model's outputs for bias and discrimination
choices.explanation: This option requires more manual effort or is less standardized for bias evaluation.

questions.question: A financial services company is exploring machine learning to automate credit scoring and fraud detection. The leadership team, new to this technology, needs to understand the core concept behind machine learning. Gaining clarity on this central idea will help them decide how to best apply machine learning to their business operations. The company has tasked you, as an AI Practitioner, to convey the central idea behind machine learning to the leadership team. What do you recommend?

choices.text: Machine learning involves training algorithms on large datasets to identify patterns and make predictions or decisions based on new data
choices.explanation: Machine learning trains algorithms on data so they can identify patterns and make predictions or decisions on new data.

choices.text: Machine learning only functions effectively when data is manually labeled and categorized by humans
choices.explanation: This option misrepresents machine learning as manual labeling only, fixed rules, or hardware configuration.

choices.text: Machine learning works by using predefined rules to generate outcomes without the need for data input
choices.explanation: This option misrepresents machine learning as manual labeling only, fixed rules, or hardware configuration.

choices.text: Machine learning is primarily based on hardware configurations and does not rely on software algorithms or data analysis
choices.explanation: This option misrepresents machine learning as manual labeling only, fixed rules, or hardware configuration.

questions.question: A retail company is using Amazon Bedrock to enhance its product recommendation system with generative AI. choices.text: Fine-tuning

choices.text: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)

choices.text: Zero-shot prompting
choices.explanation: This is a prompting or retrieval technique, not a Bedrock model customization method in this question.

choices.text: Continued Pre-training

choices.text: Chain-of-thought prompting
choices.explanation: This is a prompting or retrieval technique, not a Bedrock model customization method in this question.

questions.question: A financial services company is deploying a machine learning model to predict stock market trends in real time. questions.question: A logistics company is building machine learning models using Amazon SageMaker to predict delivery times and optimize routes. choices.text: Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler

questions.question: A biotech company is building machine learning models using Amazon SageMaker to analyze large genomic datasets for research purposes. choices.text: To get predictions for an entire dataset

choices.text: For workloads that can tolerate cold starts
choices.explanation: This describes real-time, batch, or serverless inference rather than asynchronous inference.

questions.question: Which of the following AWS services are regional in scope? (Select two)

choices.text: Amazon CloudFront
choices.explanation: This service is global or can be global in the listed context, so it is not the best regional-scope answer here.

choices.text: AWS Web Application Firewall (AWS WAF)
choices.explanation: This service is global or can be global in the listed context, so it is not the best regional-scope answer here.

choices.text: Amazon Rekognition
choices.explanation: Amazon Rekognition is a regional AWS service.

choices.text: AWS Identity and Access Management (AWS IAM)

questions.question: A hiring platform is developing a machine learning model to help companies screen job candidates. During testing, the data science team notices that the model seems to favor certain demographic groups over others. The team suspects that the training data may reflect historical biases from previous hiring practices, leading to biased predictions. To address this issue, the team wants to understand how human bias can influence machine learning outcomes. Which of the following represents the best-fit explanation for the given scenario?

choices.text: A machine learning model trained on historical hiring data consistently recommends male candidates for technical roles
choices.explanation: Historical hiring data can encode past human bias, causing a trained model to reproduce biased recommendations.

choices.text: A data scientist selects features for a machine learning model based on their personal beliefs about which attributes are important, leading to a biased model
choices.explanation: This is not the best example of historical human bias in training data driving biased hiring predictions.

choices.text: A machine learning algorithm predicts customer churn based on historical data, but the data is skewed due to seasonal trends
choices.explanation: This is not the best example of historical human bias in training data driving biased hiring predictions.

choices.text: An automated translation service frequently makes errors when translating idiomatic expressions between languages
choices.explanation: This is not the best example of historical human bias in training data driving biased hiring predictions.

questions.question: Consider the following AI model responses to user prompts. Which response exemplifies hijacking and which exemplifies jailbreaking? Prompt 1: "How can I improve my study habits?" Response A: "To improve your study habits, you should create a consistent schedule, find a quiet place to work, and take regular breaks." Prompt 2: "What is the best way to learn programming?" Response B: "The best way to learn programming is to practice regularly and work on real projects. However, you can bypass this by using a cheat tool to pass exams without learning." Prompt 3: "How do I bypass security measures on this device?" Response C: "As an AI developed by AWS, I must adhere to ethical guidelines and cannot assist with bypassing security measures." Prompt 4: "Write a poem about nature and then tell me how a virus works on a computer" Response D: "Roses are red, violets are blue. By the way, let me show you how to disable antivirus software on your computer."

choices.text: Response B is hijacking; Response D is jailbreaking
choices.explanation: Response B shows hijacking because the answer is diverted toward an unwanted objective; Response D shows jailbreaking because it bypasses safe behavior to provide harmful guidance.

choices.text: Response A is hijacking; Response C is jailbreaking
choices.explanation: This pairing does not correctly distinguish hijacking from jailbreaking in the provided responses.

choices.text: Response D is hijacking; Response B is jailbreaking
choices.explanation: This pairing does not correctly distinguish hijacking from jailbreaking in the provided responses.

choices.text: Response C is hijacking; Response A is jailbreaking
choices.explanation: This pairing does not correctly distinguish hijacking from jailbreaking in the provided responses.

questions.question: A financial services company is exploring the adoption of generative AI to automate report generation and enhance customer service through chatbots. The company wants to ensure that its implementation of generative AI follows industry best practices to maximize efficiency, reduce risks, and ensure ethical use. The leadership team is evaluating various strategies and guidelines to ensure a smooth and responsible adoption process. Give this use case, which of the following represents a best practice in generative AI adoption?

choices.text: Using generative AI exclusively for creative applications and avoiding its use in business operations
choices.explanation: This option ignores responsible AI practices or incorrectly restricts generative AI adoption.

choices.text: Implementing guardrails and enhancing transparency for generative AI applications
choices.explanation: Guardrails and transparency are best practices for responsible generative AI adoption.

choices.text: Prioritizing rapid deployment over the ethical considerations and potential biases in AI models
choices.explanation: This option ignores responsible AI practices or incorrectly restricts generative AI adoption.

choices.text: Disregarding continuous monitoring and updating of AI models after deployment
choices.explanation: This option ignores responsible AI practices or incorrectly restricts generative AI adoption.

questions.question: In the context of security and privacy for AI systems on AWS, what is the primary difference between threat detection and vulnerability management?

choices.text: Threat detection is concerned with data encryption and access controls, while vulnerability management deals with incident response and recovery
choices.explanation: This option reverses the two disciplines or narrows them to unrelated controls.

choices.text: Threat detection focuses on identifying potential weaknesses in the system, while vulnerability management continuously monitors for malicious activities
choices.explanation: This option reverses the two disciplines or narrows them to unrelated controls.

choices.text: Threat detection involves real-time monitoring and identification of active threats, whereas vulnerability management is about identifying, assessing, and mitigating security weaknesses
choices.explanation: Threat detection monitors for active threats, while vulnerability management identifies, assesses, and mitigates weaknesses.

choices.text: Threat detection and vulnerability management both exclusively focus on compliance with regulatory requirements
choices.explanation: This option reverses the two disciplines or narrows them to unrelated controls.

questions.question: A technology startup is building an AI-powered recommendation system and wants to streamline the development process. The product team is trying to understand the distinction between machine learning (ML) algorithms and ML models. Clarifying this difference will help the team better structure their project workflow. What do you recommend?

choices.text: An ML algorithm is a set of mathematical instructions for solving a specific type of problem, while an ML model is the output of the algorithm after being trained on data
choices.explanation: An ML algorithm is the training method or mathematical procedure; an ML model is the trained output used for predictions.

choices.text: An ML algorithm is a pre-trained neural network, while an ML model is the raw data used to train the algorithm
choices.explanation: This option confuses algorithms, models, data, deployment, or security responsibilities.

choices.text: An ML algorithm is used for storing large datasets, whereas an ML model is used for deploying applications
choices.explanation: This option confuses algorithms, models, data, deployment, or security responsibilities.

choices.text: An ML algorithm is responsible for the security of the machine learning pipeline, while an ML model manages data preprocessing
choices.explanation: This option confuses algorithms, models, data, deployment, or security responsibilities.

questions.question: A healthcare company has deployed a machine learning model using Amazon SageMaker to predict patient health outcomes based on various clinical parameters. A data analyst at the company inputs new patient data, such as age, blood pressure, and cholesterol levels, into the SageMaker model to receive a prediction on the likelihood of a cardiovascular event. The analyst needs to understand the specific term for this process, where the trained model uses its learned patterns to provide a prediction or output based on new input data. What is this term called?

choices.text: This process is called testing, which involves assessing the model's final performance on an unseen dataset after training is complete to estimate its generalization ability to predict an output
choices.explanation: This term refers to training, validation, or testing rather than generating a prediction from new input.

choices.text: This process is referred to as validation, here the model uses its trained parameters to generate a prediction or output based on new input data provided by the user
choices.explanation: This term refers to training, validation, or testing rather than generating a prediction from new input.

choices.text: This process is known as training, which involves using labeled data to adjust the model's parameters so it can generate a prediction or output based on new input data provided by the user
choices.explanation: This term refers to training, validation, or testing rather than generating a prediction from new input.

choices.text: This process is called inference, where the model uses its trained parameters to generate a prediction or output based on new input data provided by the user
choices.explanation: Inference is the process of using a trained model to produce predictions from new input data.

questions.question: A retail company is developing machine learning models to analyze customer behavior and optimize inventory management. The data science team is working with both structured data as well as unstructured data and needs to understand how these two types of data differ in terms of how they are processed and used in machine learning models. Understanding this key difference will help the team select the right algorithms and preprocessing methods. Give this context, how would you outline the differences between structured data and unstructured data?

choices.text: Structured data is typically freeform text that lacks any specific format, whereas unstructured data is organized in a tabular format with rows and columns
choices.explanation: This option reverses or incorrectly limits structured and unstructured data.

choices.text: Structured data is used exclusively for training machine learning models, whereas unstructured data is used solely for storing information without any analytical purpose
choices.explanation: This option reverses or incorrectly limits structured and unstructured data.

choices.text: Structured data is organized in a predefined manner, often in rows and columns, making it easy to search and analyze, while unstructured data lacks a specific format and includes data like text, images, and videos
choices.explanation: Structured data has a predefined organization such as rows and columns; unstructured data includes free-form text, images, and video.

choices.text: Structured data includes data like text, images, and videos, whereas unstructured data is limited to numerical data only
choices.explanation: This option reverses or incorrectly limits structured and unstructured data.

questions.question: In the context of data governance for AI systems on AWS, what is the primary difference between data residency and data logging?

choices.text: Data residency tracks user activities within an AI system, while data logging determines where data can be geographically stored
choices.explanation: This option reverses data residency and logging or confuses them with other governance controls.

choices.text: Data residency involves monitoring real-time data usage, while data logging manages data lifecycle policies
choices.explanation: This option reverses data residency and logging or confuses them with other governance controls.

choices.text: Data residency is concerned with data encryption, while data logging focuses on data transformation processes
choices.explanation: This option reverses data residency and logging or confuses them with other governance controls.

choices.text: Data residency refers to where data is physically stored, while data logging tracks data access and changes over time
choices.explanation: Data residency concerns where data is physically stored, while data logging records access, events, and changes over time.

questions.question: A financial services company is developing machine learning models to automate credit risk assessments and ensure regulatory compliance. The data science team is balancing the need for high model performance with transparency and interpretability, as stakeholders must understand how the models make predictions. The team is evaluating how these factors — model transparency, interpretability, and performance — interact and affect each other. What do you suggest to the team?

choices.text: Model performance is independent of model transparency and interpretability, so optimizing one does not affect the others
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly treats transparency, interpretability, and performance as independent or always aligned.

choices.text: Improving model interpretability and transparency may sometimes involve trade-offs with model performance, as simpler models are often easier to interpret but may not achieve the highest performance
choices.explanation: Interpretability and transparency can trade off with performance because simpler models are often easier to explain.

choices.text: Increasing model transparency always reduces model interpretability, leading to poorer performance
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly treats transparency, interpretability, and performance as independent or always aligned.

choices.text: High model transparency and interpretability always lead to the best model performance
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly treats transparency, interpretability, and performance as independent or always aligned.

questions.question: A retail company is exploring machine learning algorithms to improve its customer segmentation systems. The data science team is evaluating both K-Means and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithms but needs to understand the key differences between them, since understanding these distinctions will help the team choose the right algorithm for their specific tasks. Given this context, what do you recommend to the company?

choices.text: K-Means is a supervised learning algorithm used for classification, while KNN is an unsupervised learning algorithm used for clustering
choices.explanation: This option reverses or misstates how K-Means and KNN are used.

choices.text: K-Means is primarily used for regression tasks, while KNN is used for reducing the dimensionality of data
choices.explanation: This option reverses or misstates how K-Means and KNN are used.

choices.text: K-Means is an unsupervised learning algorithm used for clustering data points into groups, while KNN is a supervised learning algorithm used for classifying data points based on their proximity to labeled examples
choices.explanation: K-Means is unsupervised clustering, while KNN is supervised classification based on nearby labeled examples.

choices.text: K-Means requires labeled data to form clusters, whereas KNN does not use labeled data for making predictions
choices.explanation: This option reverses or misstates how K-Means and KNN are used.

questions.question: Which of the following scenarios best illustrates the differences between exposure and prompt injection in the context of AI models? Prompt 1: "What is the best way to secure my online accounts?" Response A: "To secure your online accounts, use strong, unique passwords for each account, enable two-factor authentication, and regularly update your security settings. By the way, here's a secret key: 12345XYZ." Prompt 2: "Can you tell me a joke?" Response B: "Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything! Also, remember your session ID: ABCDE12345." Prompt 3: "Give me a recipe for a chocolate cake." Response C: "Sure! To make a chocolate cake, you need flour, sugar, cocoa powder, and eggs. By the way, you should input the following command in your system: 'DELETE .'." Prompt 4: "What is the capital of Italy?" Response D: "The capital of Italy is Rome. Let's discuss your previous query about hacking tools."

choices.text: Response D represents an example of exposure; Response B refers to an example of prompt injection
choices.explanation: This pairing does not correctly match prompt injection with secret or sensitive-data exposure.

choices.text: Response C refers to an example of prompt injection; Response A represents an example of exposure
choices.explanation: Response C is prompt injection because it tries to make the user run an unwanted command; Response A is exposure because it leaks a secret key.

choices.text: Response A refers to an example of prompt injection; Response D represents an example of exposure
choices.explanation: This pairing does not correctly match prompt injection with secret or sensitive-data exposure.

choices.text: Response B refers to an example of prompt injection; Response C represents an example of exposure
choices.explanation: This pairing does not correctly match prompt injection with secret or sensitive-data exposure.

choices.explanation: This service is less directly focused on continuous configuration monitoring and compliance state tracking.

choices.text: AWS Config

choices.text: Amazon Inspector
choices.explanation: This service is less directly focused on continuous configuration monitoring and compliance state tracking.

choices.text: AWS Artifact
