questions.question: A retail company is deploying machine learning models to predict customer demand and optimize inventory management. The company needs to decide between using real-time inference vs batch inference. Understanding the key differences between these approaches, including their use cases, latency requirements, and processing needs, is crucial for optimizing the company's operations. Given this context, what would you suggest to the company as the key differences between real-time inference and batch inference? (Select two)

choices.text: Batch inference follows a synchronous execution mode, whereas real-time inference follows an asynchronous execution mode
choices.explanation: This option reverses real-time and batch inference characteristics.

choices.text: Real-time inference processes data in large batches at scheduled intervals, while batch inference processes individual data points immediately as they arrive
choices.explanation: This option reverses real-time and batch inference characteristics.

choices.text: Batch inference follows an API-based invocation, whereas real-time inference follows a schedule-based invocation
choices.explanation: This option reverses real-time and batch inference characteristics.

choices.text: Real-time inference is used for applications requiring immediate predictions with low latency, whereas batch inference is used for processing large volumes of data at once, often with higher latency
choices.explanation: Real-time inference is for immediate low-latency predictions, while batch inference processes larger data volumes together with higher latency.

choices.text: Real-time inference follows a synchronous execution mode, whereas batch inference follows an asynchronous execution mode
choices.explanation: Real-time inference is synchronous, while batch inference is commonly asynchronous or scheduled for offline processing.

questions.question: A tech company is leveraging generative AI to develop personalized customer experiences and is considering whether to use a pre-built Foundation Model (FM) or to customize a model tailored to their specific needs. The team needs to understand the key differences between using a Foundation Model as-is versus customizing a model with their own data to enhance performance for specific tasks. This distinction will guide their strategy for deploying the most effective AI solution. What do you suggest?

choices.text: FM is an AI model with a large number of parameters and trained on a massive amount of diverse data, whereas, model customization is the process of using training data to adjust the model parameter values in a base model to create a custom model
choices.explanation: A foundation model is broadly pre-trained on large diverse data, while customization adjusts a base model with task-specific training data.

choices.text: Model customization refers to an AI model with a large number of parameters and trained on a massive amount of diverse data, whereas, FM refers to the process of using training data to adjust the model parameter values in a base model to create a custom model
choices.explanation: This option reverses or conflates foundation models and model customization.

choices.text: Both model customization and FM refer to the process of using training data to adjust the model parameter values in a base model to create a custom model
choices.explanation: This option reverses or conflates foundation models and model customization.

choices.text: Both model customization and FM refer to an AI model with a large number of parameters and trained on a massive amount of diverse data
choices.explanation: This option reverses or conflates foundation models and model customization.

questions.question: A tech company is integrating generative AI into its customer support system to automatically answer user queries. During testing, the team notices that the AI occasionally generates responses that sound convincing but contain inaccurate information. To address this issue, the team needs to understand the phenomenon where a generative AI model produces information that may appear plausible but is factually incorrect. What is this phenomenon called?

choices.text: Fairness
choices.explanation: This term describes a different responsible AI property, not fabricated or inaccurate generated content.

choices.text: Explainability
choices.explanation: This term describes a different responsible AI property, not fabricated or inaccurate generated content.

choices.text: Controllability
choices.explanation: This term describes a different responsible AI property, not fabricated or inaccurate generated content.

choices.text: Hallucination
choices.explanation: Hallucination is when a generative AI model produces plausible-sounding but factually incorrect information.

questions.question: A financial services company is exploring the use of AI to improve fraud detection and automate credit risk assessments. The data science team is evaluating whether to use traditional machine learning techniques or deep learning, depending on the complexity of the tasks and the size of the data involved. Understanding the key differences between deep learning and traditional machine learning will help the team choose the right approach. Which of the following would you suggest to the team? (Select two)

choices.text: Deep learning models are always faster to train than traditional machine learning models, regardless of the dataset size
choices.explanation: This statement overgeneralizes training speed, learning type, or preprocessing requirements.

choices.text: In traditional machine learning, a data scientist manually determines the set of relevant features that the software must analyze, whereas in deep learning, the data scientist gives only raw data to the software and the deep learning network derives the features by itself
choices.explanation: Traditional ML often relies on manually selected features, while deep learning can learn feature representations from raw data.

choices.text: Traditional machine learning algorithms are only used for supervised learning tasks, whereas deep learning algorithms are only used for unsupervised learning tasks
choices.explanation: This statement overgeneralizes training speed, learning type, or preprocessing requirements.

choices.text: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning that uses neural networks with many layers to learn from large amounts of data, while traditional machine learning algorithms often require feature extraction and can use various methods such as decision trees or support vector machines
choices.explanation: Deep learning is a subset of ML that uses many-layer neural networks, while traditional ML uses methods such as decision trees or SVMs.

choices.text: Deep learning models do not require any data preprocessing, while traditional machine learning models require extensive data preprocessing
choices.explanation: This statement overgeneralizes training speed, learning type, or preprocessing requirements.

questions.question: A retail company is utilizing Amazon Bedrock to generate personalized product descriptions and recommendations. The data science team is experimenting with the Top K inference parameter, since it is crucial to understand how adjusting the Top K parameter impacts the responses for optimizing customer interactions. What do you suggest to the team regarding the Top K parameter?

choices.text: Specifies the sequences of characters that stop the model from generating further tokens
choices.explanation: This describes stop sequences, top-p, or temperature rather than top-k.

choices.text: Influences the number of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token
choices.explanation: Top K controls how many of the most likely next-token candidates the model considers.

choices.text: Influences the percentage of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token
choices.explanation: This describes stop sequences, top-p, or temperature rather than top-k.

choices.text: Influences the likelihood of the model selecting lower-probability outputs, thereby impacting the creativity of the model’s output
choices.explanation: This describes stop sequences, top-p, or temperature rather than top-k.

questions.question: A healthcare company is using machine learning to analyze patient data and improve diagnostics. The data science team is considering both supervised and unsupervised machine learning approaches to handle different types of data, as understanding the key differences between these two approaches will help the team determine which method is best suited for tasks like disease prediction versus discovering hidden patterns in patient data. Which of the following would you identify as the key difference between supervised machine learning and unsupervised machine learning?

choices.text: Supervised machine learning focuses on finding patterns in data without any specific guidance, while unsupervised machine learning uses labeled data to make predictions
choices.explanation: This option reverses, narrows, or incorrectly describes supervised and unsupervised learning.

choices.text: Supervised machine learning is used only for clustering tasks, whereas unsupervised machine learning is used only for regression tasks
choices.explanation: This option reverses, narrows, or incorrectly describes supervised and unsupervised learning.

choices.text: Supervised machine learning requires labeled data for training, whereas unsupervised machine learning does not use any data for training
choices.explanation: This option reverses, narrows, or incorrectly describes supervised and unsupervised learning.

choices.text: Supervised machine learning involves training models with labeled data to make predictions or classify data, whereas unsupervised machine learning identifies patterns and relationships in unlabeled data
choices.explanation: Supervised learning trains on labeled data, while unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data.

questions.question: A financial services company manages a machine learning model to assess loan eligibility for its customers. The company wants to migrate to AWS Cloud and is looking at understanding the capabilities of the various SageMaker services to operationalize and manage its Machine Learning workflow. As an AI Practitioner, what would you recommend to the company as the best-fit use case for the SageMaker Clarify service?

choices.text: You can use SageMaker Clarify to identify potential bias in data preparation, allowing you to detect and measure bias in datasets and models to ensure fairness and transparency in machine learning applications
choices.explanation: SageMaker Clarify helps detect and measure bias in datasets and models to support fairness and transparency.

choices.text: You can use SageMaker Clarify to monitor the quality of a model, which involves assessing and optimizing model performance in real-time during deployment to improve its accuracy and reliability
choices.explanation: This is a capability of another SageMaker service, not the primary use case for SageMaker Clarify.

choices.text: You can use SageMaker Clarify to automate hyperparameter tuning, which is the process of automatically optimizing the hyperparameters of a model to achieve the best performance.
choices.explanation: This is a capability of another SageMaker service, not the primary use case for SageMaker Clarify.

choices.text: You can use SageMaker Clarify to prepare ML models with no coding involved, which offers a no-code interface for building, training, and deploying machine learning models without requiring any programming skills
choices.explanation: This is a capability of another SageMaker service, not the primary use case for SageMaker Clarify.

questions.question: A financial services company is developing a machine learning model to predict credit risk and optimize loan approvals. The data science team is preparing the dataset for model development and needs to understand how to properly split the data into training, validation, and test sets. Each of these sets serves a different purpose in ensuring the model’s accuracy and generalization. Understanding the key differences between a training set, validation set, and test set will help the team implement an effective model evaluation strategy. What do you recommend to the company?

choices.text: The training set is used for training the model, the validation set is used for tuning hyperparameters and model selection, and the test set is used for evaluating the final model performance
choices.explanation: Training data fits the model, validation data tunes and selects the model, and test data evaluates final performance.

choices.text: The training set is used for tuning hyperparameters, the validation set is used for evaluating the final model performance, and the test set is used for training the model
choices.explanation: This option assigns the training, validation, and test sets to the wrong purposes.

choices.text: The training set, validation set, and test set all serve the same purpose of evaluating the model performance
choices.explanation: This option assigns the training, validation, and test sets to the wrong purposes.

choices.text: The training set is used for evaluating model performance, the validation set is used for training the model, and the test set is used for hyperparameter tuning
choices.explanation: This option assigns the training, validation, and test sets to the wrong purposes.

questions.question: A company wants to improve the performance of a Foundation Model (FM) being used in Amazon Bedrock. Which of the following lists the underlying techniques in the increasing order of complexity for implementing a solution?

choices.text: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), Fine-tuning, Prompt engineering
choices.explanation: This order does not reflect the usual increasing implementation complexity for improving an FM.

choices.text: Prompt engineering, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), Fine-tuning
choices.explanation: Prompt engineering is usually the simplest change, RAG adds external retrieval architecture, and fine-tuning is typically more complex.

choices.text: Prompt engineering, Fine-tuning, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
choices.explanation: This order does not reflect the usual increasing implementation complexity for improving an FM.

choices.text: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), Prompt engineering, Fine-tuning
choices.explanation: This order does not reflect the usual increasing implementation complexity for improving an FM.

questions.question: You are working as an NLP engineer at a tech company tasked with building an advanced text summarization tool to help customers generate concise summaries of lengthy documents. After successfully training your model, your manager asks you to evaluate its performance and quality to ensure it meets the required standards for deployment. Considering the nature of text summarization, which evaluation method would be most appropriate to assess the model's output effectively?

choices.text: Conduct code review to analyze the implementation code for logical or structural issues
choices.explanation: This method is less directly suited to automated evaluation of summarization output quality.

choices.text: Test the model on established benchmark datasets to evaluate performance
choices.explanation: This method is less directly suited to automated evaluation of summarization output quality.

choices.text: Use automated testing via metrics like ROUGE or BLEU to measure the similarity between generated and reference summaries
choices.explanation: ROUGE and BLEU only measure n-gram overlap with reference summaries and miss important quality dimensions like coherence, relevance, and readability.

choices.text: Leverage human evaluation to assess the quality of summaries
choices.explanation: Human evaluation is the gold standard for assessing summarization quality, capturing coherence, relevance, and readability that automated metrics like ROUGE/BLEU cannot measure.

questions.question: In the context of the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, which statement best describes the security responsibilities of both AWS and the customer when using Amazon Bedrock for generative AI applications?

choices.text: The customer is responsible for the entire security stack, including the underlying infrastructure and the AI models
choices.explanation: This option misstates the shared responsibility model by assigning all security to one party or reversing roles.

choices.text: AWS is responsible for the security of the AI models and customer data, while the customer is responsible for securing the physical infrastructure
choices.explanation: This option misstates the shared responsibility model by assigning all security to one party or reversing roles.

choices.text: AWS is responsible for securing the infrastructure that runs Amazon Bedrock, while the customer is responsible for securing their data and managing access controls
choices.explanation: AWS secures the infrastructure running Bedrock, while customers secure their data, identities, permissions, and application controls.

choices.text: AWS handles all aspects of security for Amazon Bedrock, relieving the customer of any security responsibilities
choices.explanation: This option misstates the shared responsibility model by assigning all security to one party or reversing roles.

questions.question: A healthcare company is considering using Amazon Bedrock to develop AI solutions that handle sensitive patient data, such as medical records and diagnostic information. Given the strict regulatory requirements in healthcare, the company needs to ensure that Amazon Bedrock provides robust data security and compliance features. The company is evaluating the platform's capabilities to safeguard data and meet compliance standards like HIPAA. Which of the following is correct regarding the data security and compliance aspects of Amazon Bedrock for the given use case?

choices.text: The company's data is used to improve the base Foundation Models (FMs) and it is also shared with the model providers for model optimization
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly says customer data is used for base model improvement or shared with model providers.

choices.text: The company's data is only used to improve the base Foundation Models (FMs), however, it is not shared with any model providers
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly says customer data is used for base model improvement or shared with model providers.

choices.text: The company's data is not used to improve the base Foundation Models (FMs), however, it is shared with the model providers for model optimization
choices.explanation: This option incorrectly says customer data is used for base model improvement or shared with model providers.

choices.text: The company's data is not used to improve the base Foundation Models (FMs) and it is not shared with any model providers
choices.explanation: Amazon Bedrock does not use customer data to improve base foundation models and does not share it with model providers.

questions.question: The product team at a media company needs to understand the key distinctions between the tasks performed by Natural Language Processing (NLP) compared to those performed by Computer Vision. This will help the team apply the right AI tools for different types of content. Which of the following do you suggest to the team?

choices.text: NLP and Computer Vision are both used exclusively for speech recognition tasks
choices.explanation: This option reverses or incorrectly limits NLP and computer vision tasks.

choices.text: NLP is used for analyzing and generating human language, such as text and speech, while Computer Vision is used for interpreting and understanding visual information from images and videos
choices.explanation: NLP works with human language such as text and speech, while computer vision interprets images and video.

choices.text: NLP is used for tasks such as image recognition and object detection, while Computer Vision is used for text generation and sentiment analysis
choices.explanation: This option reverses or incorrectly limits NLP and computer vision tasks.

choices.text: NLP and Computer Vision are both used for creating 3D models from textual descriptions
choices.explanation: This option reverses or incorrectly limits NLP and computer vision tasks.

questions.question: A technology company is planning to implement machine learning to improve its product recommendation system and optimize supply chain management. The data science team is evaluating different types of machine learning approaches. Gaining a clear understanding of these types will help them choose the right strategy for model development. What of the following option would you suggest to the team as the three main types of machine learning?

choices.text: Reinforcement Learning, Transfer Learning, Semi-supervised Learning
choices.explanation: This option does not include the two foundational categories of supervised and unsupervised learning.

choices.text: Transfer Learning, Semi-supervised Learning, Self-supervised Learning
choices.explanation: This option does not include the two foundational categories of supervised and unsupervised learning.

choices.text: Deep Learning, Self-supervised Learning, Reinforcement Learning
choices.explanation: This option does not include the two foundational categories of supervised and unsupervised learning.

choices.text: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Deep Learning
choices.explanation: This is the closest listed option because it includes supervised and unsupervised learning, but the fully correct triad is supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning.
